console.c(3) Library Functions Manual console.c(3)

NAME

console.c - Browser console logging for WASM via Emscripten.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


EM_JS (void, js_console_log,(int level, const char *message), { const msg=UTF8ToString(message);const levelNames=['DEV', 'DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR', 'FATAL'];const levelName=(level >=0 &&level<=5) ? levelNames[level] :'???^;const msgLen=msg.length;const hasTimestamp=msg.startsWith('[') &&msg.includes(':') &&msg.includes('.') &&msg[msg.indexOf(']')+1]===' ';const debugInfo=`[WASM_MSG_LEN=${msgLen}, HAS_TS=${hasTimestamp}]`;switch(level) { case 0:case 1:case 2:console.log(debugInfo, msg);break;case 3:console.warn(debugInfo, msg);break;case 4:case 5:console.error(debugInfo, msg);break;default:console.log(debugInfo, msg);} })
void platform_log_hook (log_level_t level, const char *message)
int wasm_parse_log_level (const uint8_t *buf, size_t count)
Parse log level from formatted message. void wasm_log_to_console (int fd, const uint8_t *buf, size_t count)
Route output to browser console based on log level.

Detailed Description

Browser console logging for WASM via Emscripten.

Definition in file console.c.

Function Documentation

EM_JS (void , js_console_log , (int level, const char *message) , { const msg=UTF8ToString(message);const levelNames=['DEV', 'DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARN', 'ERROR', 'FATAL'];const levelName=(level >=0 &&level<=5) ? levelNames[level] :'???^;const msgLen=msg.length;const hasTimestamp=msg.startsWith('[') &&msg.includes(':') &&msg.includes('.') &&msg[msg.indexOf(']')+1]===' ';const debugInfo=`[WASM_MSG_LEN=${msgLen}, HAS_TS=${hasTimestamp}]`;switch(level) { case 0:case 1:case 2:console.log(debugInfo, msg);break;case 3:console.warn(debugInfo, msg);break;case 4:case 5:console.error(debugInfo, msg);break;default:console.log(debugInfo, msg);} } )

void platform_log_hook (log_level_t level, const char * message)

Definition at line 83 of file console.c.

83                                                                {
84   if (message) {
85     // Strip ANSI codes for clean browser console output
86     static char clean_message[4096];
87     strip_ansi_codes(message, clean_message, sizeof(clean_message));
88     js_console_log((int)level, clean_message);
89   }
90 }








void
  wasm_log_to_console (int fd, const uint8_t * buf, size_t
  count)


Route output to browser console based on log level. Parses the log
    level from the message format [LEVEL] and routes to the appropriate console
    method (console.debug, console.log, etc).




Parameters


fd File descriptor (typically 1 for stdout, 2 for
  stderr)


buf Message buffer


count Message length


Definition at line 145 of file console.c.


145                                                                    {
146   // For WASM, route stdout/stderr to browser console
147   if ((fd == 1 || fd == 2) && buf && count > 0) {
148     // Parse log level from formatted message
149     int level = wasm_parse_log_level(buf, count);
150     if (level >= 0) {
151       // Create null-terminated string for console output
152       // Copy buffer to local array and null-terminate
153       static char msg_buffer[4096];
154       size_t copy_len = (count < sizeof(msg_buffer) - 1) ? count : (sizeof(msg_buffer) - 1);
155       memcpy(msg_buffer, buf, copy_len);
156       msg_buffer[copy_len] = '\0';
157 
158       // Remove trailing newline if present (console adds it automatically)
159       if (copy_len > 0 && msg_buffer[copy_len - 1] == '\n') {
160         msg_buffer[copy_len - 1] = '\0';
161       }
162 
163       // Route to console
164       js_console_log(level, msg_buffer);
165     }
166   }
167 }






References wasm_parse_log_level().


Referenced by platform_write().






int
  wasm_parse_log_level (const uint8_t * buf, size_t count)


Parse log level from formatted message. Expects format:
    "[LEVEL] message..."




Parameters


buf Message buffer


count Message length


Returns


log_level_t enum value (0-5), or -1 if not
  found


Definition at line 94 of file console.c.


94                                                            {
95   if (!buf || count < 5) {
96     return -1; // Too short to contain [LEVEL]
97   }
98 
99   // Check if message starts with [
100   if (buf[0] != '[') {
101     return -1;
102   }
103 
104   // Look for closing ]
105   int bracket_end = -1;
106   for (size_t i = 1; i < count && i < 10; i++) {
107     if (buf[i] == ']') {
108       bracket_end = (int)i;
109       break;
110     }
111   }
112 
113   if (bracket_end < 3) {
114     return -1; // Too short, minimum is [XX]
115   }
116 
117   // Extract level string
118   int level_len = bracket_end - 1;
119   const char *level_start = (const char *)&buf[1];
120 
121   // Match against known level strings (from logging.c)
122   if (level_len == 3 && strncmp(level_start, "DEV", 3) == 0) {
123     return LOG_DEV;
124   }
125   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "DEBUG", 5) == 0) {
126     return LOG_DEBUG;
127   }
128   if (level_len == 4 && strncmp(level_start, "INFO", 4) == 0) {
129     return LOG_INFO;
130   }
131   if (level_len == 4 && strncmp(level_start, "WARN", 4) == 0) {
132     return LOG_WARN;
133   }
134   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "ERROR", 5) == 0) {
135     return LOG_ERROR;
136   }
137   if (level_len == 5 && strncmp(level_start, "FATAL", 5) == 0) {
138     return LOG_FATAL;
139   }
140 
141   return -1; // Level not recognized
142 }






References LOG_DEBUG, LOG_DEV, LOG_ERROR,
    LOG_FATAL, LOG_INFO, and LOG_WARN.


Referenced by wasm_log_to_console().
